title: Kamteswari temple
tagline: Kamteswari temple — spiritual town of West Bengal

overview.short: Kamteswari Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Hindu goddess Kamteswari.
overview.description: Kamteswari Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Hindu goddess Kamteswari. It is located at Gosanimari in the Dinhata I CD block in the Dinhata subdivision of the Cooch Behar district in West Bengal, India.

topPlaces.name: Rajpat
topPlaces.description: Rajpat or Kamtapur Fort is an archaeological site in present-day Gosanimari in the Dinhata I CD block in the Dinhata subdivision of the Cooch Behar district in West Bengal, India.

topPlaces.name: Dinhata I
topPlaces.description: Dinhata I is a community development block (CD block) that forms an administrative division in the Dinhata subdivision of the Cooch Behar district in the Indian state of West Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Sitai
topPlaces.description: Sitai is a community development block (CD block) that forms an administrative division in the Dinhata subdivision of the Cooch Behar district in the Indian state of West Bengal.

topPlaces.name: British expedition to Tibet
topPlaces.description: The British expedition to Tibet, also known as the Younghusband expedition, began in December 1903 and lasted until September 1904. The expedition was effectively a temporary invasion by British Indian Armed Forces under the auspices of the Tibet Frontier Commission, whose purported mission was to establish diplomatic relations and resolve the dispute over the border between Tibet and Sikkim.

items.note: Rajpat or Kamtapur Fort is an archaeological site in present-day Gosanimari in the Dinhata I CD block in the Dinhata subdivision of the Cooch Behar district in West Bengal, India.
Dinhata I is a community development block (CD block) that forms an administrative division in the Dinhata subdivision of the Cooch Behar district in the Indian state of West Bengal.
Sitai is a community development block (CD block) that forms an administrative division in the Dinhata subdivision of the Cooch Behar district in the Indian state of West Bengal.

The British expedition to Tibet, also known as the Younghusband expedition, began in December 1903 and lasted until September 1904. The expedition was effectively a temporary invasion by British Indian Armed Forces under the auspices of the Tibet Frontier Commission, whose purported mission was to establish diplomatic relations and resolve the dispute over the border between Tibet and Sikkim.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kamteswari temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kamteswari temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kamteswari temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Bagdogra Airport (Siliguri/Darjeeling) (120 km) and the nearest railway station is Alipurduar Junction (42 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kamteswari temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kamteswari temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kamteswari temple?
faq.a: Stays in Kamteswari temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kamteswari temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Rajpat, Dinhata I, Sitai, British expedition to Tibet.
